notes: KNOWN FAILING — Gemini CLI v0.46.0 genuinely supports background processes (run_shell_command with is_background:true returns immediately with a 'Background process started with PID' result; a 'List Background Processes' tool polls to exit). But the geminicli adapter (core/adapters/inbound/agents/geminicli/parser.go) has NO background-process tracking: it settles the session to ready as soon as the assistant message carries final text and opens no tool calls, so it does NOT hold the session working while a detached process is alive. The BackgroundProcessCount + HasLiveBackgroundProcess working-hold is wired only from the claudecode backgroundTaskId / tasks-output shape Gemini does not produce. A recording is therefore expected to fail bg_alive_working and turn_end: the session flips working→ready at the turn's normal end while the background process is still alive. The spec asserts the CORRECT arc (ready→working, an extended working dwell that does NOT flip to ready while the background process is alive, then a single working→ready once it exits) — fix is the Gemini parallel of #450/#445 in the geminicli parser; re-record and clear known_failing then.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after agent launch
User prompt drives the session ready → working

While the run_shell_command is_background process is alive, the session stays working past the turn's end — it does NOT settle to ready until the background process exits (KNOWN FAILING: the geminicli adapter does not yet track background processes, so the session settles to ready here)
After the background process exits (the list-background poll reports it gone and the liveness probe sees no live writer), the session settles working → ready
